# Input Manifest

## Purpose

`input-manifest.json` is the first machine-readable summary of a business input package.

It does not normalize the business yet.
It only answers:

- what files are here
- what they seem to be
- what is missing
- what looks wrong

## Current Location

Example:

- [input-manifest.json](C:/Users/Sergi/OneDrive/Escritorio/Webs/business-input/the-dosa-spot/normalized/input-manifest.json)

## Generated By

- `npm run ingest:manifest -- <slug>`

Current script:

- [generate-input-manifest.mjs](C:/Users/Sergi/OneDrive/Escritorio/Webs/scripts/generate-input-manifest.mjs)

## What It Contains

- `seed`
  - parsed `intake.json` when present
- `discoveredFiles`
  - every non-dotfile found under `raw/`
- `expectedInputs`
  - whether the package has seed, maps link, HTML, images, docs, and notes
- `validations`
  - hard and soft issues
- `summary`
  - counts and quick package health

## Classification Rules

The current manifest classifies files by:

- folder
- file extension
- filename prefix

Examples:

- `maps-link.txt` -> `maps-link`
- HTML inside `raw/maps/` -> `maps-html`
- HTML inside `raw/html/` -> `page-html`
- `hero-main.jpg` -> `image-hero`
- `dish-dosa.jpg` -> `image-dish`
- files in `raw/docs/` -> `document-*`
- files in `raw/notes/` -> `notes`

## Validations Implemented

- missing `intake.json`
- missing slug
- folder slug mismatch with `intake.json`
- missing business name
- no HTML sources
- no images
- repeated filenames
- chaotic naming
- unsupported or unclear files
- wrong placement outside the expected raw folders

## Practical Reading

The manifest is not a final truth file.

Use it to decide:

- whether the intake package is ready to normalize
- which missing inputs actually matter
- whether filenames need cleanup
- whether another pass of collection is needed before moving on
